name: Glenn Gould
abstract: Glenn Herbert Gould (; né Gold; 25 September 1932 – 4 October 1982) was a Canadian pianist and broadcaster. Regarded as one of the greatest pianists of the 20th century, he was renowned as an interpreter of the keyboard works of Johann Sebastian Bach.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, especially when Glen Gould plays it. [INTERVIEWEE] The Brandenburg concertos, if people know what they’re doing, that swings out of the room.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: You’re back to “You just finished the first act.” You’re doing it by rote. There’s only so much you can do with a piece called Bedford Drive or whatever the piece may be. After that you say, Glen Gould must have known that when he walked away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: If I could have done what Glenn Gould did, go into a studio and work on one piece ’til it’s good and then go away and leave it alone and never play it again, that’s fine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
